reasoning: The resolution requires the number of 'Protests' in the preceding 30 days to exceed 10 times the baseline value of 38, meaning >380 protests must occur. Historically, the highest number of ALL event types (including riots, violence, etc.) recorded by ACLED in a single month in Tunisia was around 178 in January 2016. A spike to >380 protests alone would represent a massive, unprecedented nationwide uprising far exceeding recent historical peaks. Current news from Tunisia shows no evidence of such an extraordinary mass movement as of May 25, 2025. Generating this many events would require an average of roughly 12-13 distinct protests every day for 30 consecutive days. Thus, the near-term probability is essentially zero. I am placing the probabilities at the allowed lower limit (0.05) for the near dates, scaling up marginally to 0.08 for November 2025 to account for the slight chance of a sudden massive revolt in the autumn.
